name: real-llama-cpp-python

metadata.name: real-llama-cpp-python
metadata.summary: A simple custom LLM wrapper for llama.cpp with LangChain compatibility.

documentation:
# real-llama-cpp-python
A simple custom wrapper for llama.cpp models in Python, support seamlessly interaction with LangChain.

## Background
Installing llama-cpp-python is a pain in the bum. However, langchain only supports llama-cpp-python. I had a long Xmas leave and couldn't install the latest llama-cpp-python to run the quantized models from the latest llama.cpp. I decided to create my own version of real-llama-cpp-python to be:wq compatible with LangChain. 

## Installation

```bash
pip install custom-llm

classes.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp

methods.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp._default_params
documentation: Get the default parameters for calling llama_cpp.
n: int
"""The number of characters from the last message of the prompt to be echoed."""

methods.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp._call
documentation:
Run the LLM on the given input.

Override this method to implement the LLM logic.

Args:
    prompt: The prompt to generate from.
    stop: Stop words to use when generating. Model output is cut off at the
        first occurrence of any of the stop substrings.
        If stop tokens are not supported consider raising NotImplementedError.
    run_manager: Callback manager for the run.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary additional keyword arguments. These are usually passed
        to the model provider API call.

Returns:
    The model output as a string. Actual completions SHOULD NOT include the prompt.

methods.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp._stream
documentation:
Stream the LLM on the given prompt.

This method should be overridden by subclasses that support streaming.

If not implemented, the default behavior of calls to stream will be to
fallback to the non-streaming version of the model and return
the output as a single chunk.

Args:
    prompt: The prompt to generate from.
    stop: Stop words to use when generating. Model output is cut off at the
        first occurrence of any of these substrings.
    run_manager: Callback manager for the run.
    **kwargs: Arbitrary additional keyword arguments. These are usually passed
        to the model provider API call.

Returns:
    An iterator of GenerationChunks.

documentation: : :meta private:

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.model_path
documentation: REQUIRED: The path to the Llama model file.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.lora_base
documentation: The path to the Llama LoRA base model.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.lora_path
documentation: The path to the Llama LoRA. If None, no LoRa is loaded.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.n_ctx
documentation: Token context size, Default 512

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.n_parts
documentation:
Number of parts to split the model into.
If -1, the number of parts is automatically determined.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.seed
documentation: Seed. If -1, a random seed is used.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.logits_all
documentation: Return logits for all tokens, not just the last token.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.vocab_only
documentation: Only load the vocabulary, no weights.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.use_mlock
documentation: Force system to keep model in RAM.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.n_threads
documentation:
Number of threads to use.
If None, the number of threads is automatically determined.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.n_batch
documentation:
Number of tokens to process in parallel.
Should be a number between 1 and n_ctx.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.n_gpu_layers
documentation: Number of layers to be loaded into gpu memory. Default None.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.suffix
documentation: A suffix to append to the generated text. If None, no suffix is appended.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.max_tokens
documentation: The maximum number of tokens to generate.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.temperature
documentation: The temperature to use for sampling.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.top_p
documentation: The top-p value to use for sampling.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.logprobs
documentation: The number of logprobs to return. If None, no logprobs are returned.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.echo
documentation: Whether to echo the prompt.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.stop
documentation: A list of strings to stop generation when encountered.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.repeat_penalty
documentation: The penalty to apply to repeated tokens.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.top_k
documentation: The top-k value to use for sampling.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.use_mmap
documentation: Whether to keep the model loaded in RAM

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.rope_freq_scale
documentation: Scale factor for rope sampling.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.rope_freq_base
documentation: Base frequency for rope sampling.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.model_kwargs
documentation: Any additional parameters to pass to llama.cpp

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.streaming
documentation: Whether to stream the results, token by token.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.grammar_path
documentation:
grammar_path: Path to the .gbnf file that defines formal grammars
for constraining model outputs. For instance, the grammar can be used
to force the model to generate valid JSON or to speak exclusively in emojis. At most
one of grammar_path and grammar should be passed in.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.grammar
documentation:
grammar: formal grammar for constraining model outputs. For instance, the grammar 
can be used to force the model to generate valid JSON or to speak exclusively in 
emojis. At most one of grammar_path and grammar should be passed in.

class_variables.name: llama_cpp.model.CustomLlamaCpp.verbose
documentation: Print verbose output to stderr.

llama.cpp model.

To use, you should have the llama.cpp library installed (no need to install llama-cpp-python), 
Check out: https://github.com/ggerganov/llama.cpp
